headline: Nickolas Asset Management Buys Resin Processing Facility From Material Difference Technologies
summary_text: Nickolas Asset Management Buys Resin Processing Facility From Material Difference Technologies ×Search Pulse 2.0** # Nickolas Asset Management Buys Resin Processing Facility From Material Difference Technologies By Amit Chowdhry •Feb 14, 2026 Pulse 2.0 Nickolas Asset Management Buys Resin Processing Facility From Material Difference Technologies Follow Pulse 2.0 on LinkedIn Nickolas Asset Management (NAM) has acquired a resin processing and materials facility from Material Difference Technologies (MDT), a Florida-based supplier and processor of plastic resins serving industrial and consumer manufacturing markets. The deal adds a 120,000-square-foot operation with the ability to process millions of pounds of reprocessed plastic resins annually, expanding NAM’s materials platform and reinforcing the firm’s emphasis on asset- and infrastructure-heavy manufacturing. The acquired site combines resin distribution with formulating, blending, and compounding capabilities that NAM said will support its broader manufacturing platform with tailored material solutions aligned to specific product, performance, and customer requirements. In addition to production capacity, the facility includes technical development support, quality assurance, and in-house analytical laboratory services. NAM positioned the deal as a step toward deeper vertical integration, aiming to increase control over materials, quality, cost, and supply chain reliability across the businesses it owns and operates. By bringing materials processing closer to finished-product manufacturing, the firm expects to improve consistency and responsiveness while ... The facility is dedicated to recycling and reprocessing post-consumer and post-industrial plastic scrap into reusable resin pellets, a process NAM said diverts tens of millions of pounds of plastic annually from landfills. NAM added that the recycled resin product lines produced at the site will hold multiple third-party certifications, including SCS ... relationships, and operations. NAM described itself as an evergreen, operator-led family office built on permanent capital and a long-term ownership philosophy. Following the acquisition, the firm said it maintains a national footprint of eight manufacturing facilities totaling approximately 2.5 million square feet of production space across the United States. **KEY QUOTES:****** *“This was a business where the owner was ready for retirement and needed a thoughtful exit. headline: DuPont Introduces DuPont™ AmberLite™ FPA57 Resin to Boost Efficiency in Organic Acid Purification
summary_text: DuPont Introduces DuPont™ AmberLite™ FPA57 Resin to Boost Efficiency in Organic Acid Purification Accessibility Statement Skip Navigation Upgraded weak base anion resin delivers enhanced operating capacity, longer run lengths, and greater fouling resistance to support cost-efficient production of lactic and citric acids WILMINGTON, Del., Jan. 28, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- DuPont (NYSE: DD) today announced the launch of DuPont™ AmberLite™ FPA57 resin, an innovative weak base anion resin designed to support the growing needs of organic acid manufacturers. Organic acids play a critical role across the food and beverage, personal care, biodegradable plastics, and pharmaceutical industries. They are essential in processes ranging from pH adjustment and preservation to chemical ... As the global organic acids market expands, producers are seeking purification solutions that increase throughput while reducing operational costs. DuPont™ AmberLite™ FPA57 resin provides five to ten percent longer cycle times, helping manufacturers extend run lengths, reduce downtime, and improve productivity. Its enhanced total exchange capacity and acrylic gel matrix provide greater physical stability and fouling resistance, supporting longer resin life and more consistent deashing performance even under demanding operating conditions. Designed as an upgrade to DuPont™ AmberLite™ FPA55 resin, the new resin matches the same particle size and hydraulic characteristics, allowing customers to seamlessly upgrade and realize performance gains without adjusting system design or flow rates. DuPont™ AmberLite™ FPA57 resin also maintains the high yield demanded in lactic and citric acid processing, providing reliable removal of weak acids, color bodies, and mineral impurities to achieve consistent product quality. DuPont™ AmberLite™ FPA57 resin expands DuPont's broad portfolio of ion exchange resins, polymeric adsorbents, and membrane technologies for food and beverage applications. About DuPont DuPont (NYSE: DD) is a global innovation leader, providing advanced solutions that help transform industries and improve everyday life across our key markets of healthcare, water, construction, and transportation. More information about the company, its businesses and solutions can be found at www.dupont.com . Investors can access ... headline: After months of stability, polyethylene prices move higher
summary_text: Prices for polyethylene resins in North America are up an average of 5 cents per pound since Jan. 1, according to market sources. The last notable change occurred in June, when prices rose by 3 cents per pound, reversing 3 cent decline the prior month. Since then, the market had remained largely flat.

headline: Invista partners with Epoch Biodesign to chemically recycle nylon 66
summary_text: Together, Invista and Epoch Biodesign plan to produce customer-validated, virgin-quality recycled nylon 66 at commercial scale. Under the agreement, Epoch's enzymes will be used to break down nylon waste into its original monomers, which can then be repolymerised by Invista into new nylon 66. Technical assessments and polymer qualification are already underway, with application performance testing planned as the next phase. " When your recycling technology is chosen from a global set of options by a company whose heritage is directly linked to the invention of nylon 66, it is clearly a defining moment ," said Epoch Biodesign CEO Jacob Nathan. " We are excited to increase momentum towards our goal of global deployment; this is a clear message that one of the largest producers of nylon 66 believes in our technology and the need for effective, enzymatic recycling at commercial scale.

By Grace Vance Published: Feb. 26, 2026 at 1:29 PM CST Email This Link Share on Facebook Share on X (formerly Twitter) ... CEDAR RAPIDS, Iowa (KCRG) - Iowa is launching a new film rebate program nearly two decades after its last incentive program ended in scandal. The two-year pilot program offers up to 30% back on qualifying expenses. To qualify, productions must come from Iowa studios, carry a budget of at least $1 million, film mostly in the state, and be made available to the public. The Iowa Economic Development Authority said the Renovo Media Group in Clear Lake, Iowa proposed the ... It is Iowa’s first major rebate program since the state abruptly ended its previous incentive program in 2009 after millions of dollars were misused. The state said the new program has more safeguards against fraud, including specifying allowable and non-allowable expenses. It also requires a production’s final budget be reviewed by a third-party before approval. Eric Dean Freese, director of the Cedar Rapids Independent Film Festival, said the program represents progress but leaves out Iowa’s independent filmmakers. “It’s baby steps,” Freese said. He said the new program is not competitive enough with other states. ... The new rebate program comes as the state recently put its Greenlight Grant for low-budget films on pause — ... The state said it will be evaluating the Greenlight Grant program “to ensure it has met the goals to advance Iowa’s ... Freese said the film represents Iowa’s growing industry. He said filmmakers just need more resources. ... In a statement to TV9, the Iowa Economic Development Authority said it hopes the program will support large-scale ... So far, it says it’s received four applications for the rebate program. *Copyright 2026 KCRG. Missouri Film Office director Andrea Sporcic Klund, told Missourinet that it’s a great number.![Andrea Sporcic Klund, Missouri Film Office Director. Photo courtesy and provided by Andrea Sporcic ... Andrea Sporcic Klund, Missouri Film Office Director. Photo courtesy and provided by Andrea Sporcic Klund. “I think in relation to how much tax credit was allocated for that 40 million only 15.7 million,” said Klund. “So that ... Klund said those projects ranged from feature films and documentaries to two dozen commercials shot across the state. She said the ease of filming keeps film crews coming back to the Show-Me State. “They’re able to hire the crews,” said Klund. “The support companies are here. They’re just enjoying ... Klund told Missourinet that Missouri seems to have become a hotspot for Christmas movies as well. “I would say that tax credit has something to do with it, but I also think that we’re in the Midwest, that’s like relatable people,” said Klund. “These are the people that are actually watching these Christmas ... Klund said the tax credits showed a great return on investment for the state in 2025. “That’s 54 projects over the course of one year. So, we were very close to allocating the full cap. It’s 16 million that we have total. I do think in 2026, we will allocate all of our funding,” said Klund. In 2023, the Missouri Legislature passed and then-Gov. Mike Parson signed a bill into law to resurrect the offering of film tax credits. They are set to expire at the end of 2029, and Klund said she’s hopeful lawmakers will extend them. headline: Statewide film series celebrates Ohio’s cinema legacy to mark America’s 250th anniversary - cleveland.com
summary_text: Statewide film series celebrates Ohio’s cinema legacy to mark America’s 250th anniversary - cleveland.com Family Sharing is here! Subscribe to Premium Access for $5 # Statewide film series celebrates Ohio’s cinema legacy to mark America’s 250th anniversary - Published: Feb. 10, 2026, 5:24 p.m. - Updated: Feb. 10, 2026, 5:27 p.m. Gov. Mike DeWine and his wife, Fran, will attend the kickoff screening of "Frankenstein" in Milan on Wednesday, launching a roughly 250-day celebration of Ohio's cinema legacy. (John Kuntz, cleveland.com)John Kuntz, cleveland.com MILAN, Ohio — As part of America’s 250th birthday, Ohio is kicking off statewide film series noting the state’s contributions to the silver screen. Gov. Mike DeWine and his wife, Fran, plan to attend the first event in “Ohio Goes to the Movies” on Wednesday in Milan, the birthplace and childhood home of Thomas Edison. The opening event will screen the film “Frankenstein,” produced by Edison’s company. The choice is a nod to Edison, who with his staff developed the Kinetoscope, an early motion picture exhibition device. Edison’s birthday is also on Wednesday. The Ohio Goes to the Movie series will feature films made in Ohio, starring Ohio-born actors, set in Ohio locations, or centered on Ohioans. The series will run from mid-February through October 2026 — approximately 250 days. Ohio also has a rich history of producing leaders in the film industry. Among those are actors Clark Gable, Lillian Gish, Dorothy Dandridge, Doris Day, Paul Newman, Halle Berry, Martin Sheen and Sarah Jessica Parker. Director, producer, and screenwriter Steven Spielberg was born in Cincinnati. Countless films have been shot in Ohio, including, “Shawshank Redemption,” “Deerhunter,” “A Christmas Story,” and “The Avengers.” More recently, “Superman,” released in 2025, was shot in Cleveland. Screenings for the film series will take place in cities and towns across Ohio and include a wide range of genres, from smaller independent films to Hollywood blockbusters. A full program guide can be found online . The kickoff screening begins at 2:30 p.m. Wednesday at Milan Township Hall, 24 Park Street, second floor ballroom. ... headline: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News
summary_text: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News ****Menu Reel Chicago ****Menu Reel Chicago * Reel Indie # The Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections * **By Reel Chicago * January 15, 2026 ** ** ** ** ### Share this: * Click to share on X (Opens in new window)X * Click to share on Facebook (Opens in new window)Facebook * Click to share on LinkedIn (Opens in new window)LinkedIn * Click to share on Mail (Opens in new window)Mail * Chicago’s creative community is about to gain a new kind of gathering space, one built around collaboration, sustainability, and shared stewardship.**The Midwest Exchange**, presented by**Kinowerks and Midwest Film Festival**, is launching as a values-driven initiative designed to keep professional creative resources in motion. The concept is simple but powerful: keeping equipment, materials, and knowledge from sitting idle or going to waste, and instead create a trusted environment where filmmakers and creative professionals can connect, exchange, and reinvest in one another. At its core, the Midwest Exchange emphasizes stewardship over ownership, sustainability in practice, professional care, and long-term investment in community. The goal is not just to host an event, but to strengthen the creative ecosystem ... The partnership brings together two organizations with complementary strengths. Midwest Film Festival contributes more than two decades of nonprofit experience and credibility within the filmmaking community. Kinowerks provides purpose-built creative infrastructure, including screening rooms, studios, and thoughtfully designed event space tailored for production and collaboration. The Midwest Exchange is designed for working filmmakers, technicians, producers, directors, cinematographers, editors, post supervisors, theater and audio professionals, as well as emerging creatives committed to long-term careers. ... **The inaugural Midwest Exchange will take place February 21, 2026, from 12–4 p.m. at Kinowerks, 5645 North Ravenswood Avenue in Chicago.** Organizers estimate the initiative will reach a substantial regional audience, supported by a combined community that ... organizations’ programming. That built-in network gives the Exchange the potential to extend its impact well beyond a ... For more information or to get involved, contact**Grover Derupe atgrover.derupe@beesonventures.com**. **ALSO READ:** **31 Candles reaches theaters with Hannah Welever producing** 31 Candles ### Share this: * Click to share on X (Opens in new window)X ... * Click to share on Mail (Opens in new window)Mail * ### *Related* ... ... headline: ICA Marketplace Announces Significant First Year Results - Digital Cinema Report
summary_text: ICA Marketplace Announces Significant First Year Results - Digital Cinema Report -- The Independent Cinema Alliance, the cinema trade organization representing approximately 5,000 independently operated movie screens across the United States and Canada, today announced significant results from its Marketplace initiative, delivered in partnership with Fandango ClubBuy, designed to leverage the combined purchasing power of ICA members to secure meaningful cost savings on essential theatre products and services. The ICA Marketplace enables members to access national account pricing, rebates, and value-added programs across a wide range of mission-critical categories, including concessions, food and beverage, janitorial supplies, technology, seating, and other core operating essentials. Collectively, ICA members operate nearly 5,000 screens across North America, effectively making the ICA the third-largest cinema circuit in North America. By aggregating this unmatched independent exhibitor scale, the Marketplace delivers pricing traditionally available only to large national circuits. In 2025, the ICA completed its first full year of partnership with Fandango ClubBuy, which serves as the Marketplace’s group purchasing organization and part of Versant Media Group, marking a significant milestone for the program. Through this partnership, the ICA successfully secured national contracts with US Foods and Vistar for concession and foodservice supplies, as well as exclusive volume agreements for Orville Redenbacher popcorn and Catania popcorn oil. Participating ICA members purchased more than $13 million in food, beverage, and supplies through the program in its first year, realizing nearly 20 percent savings compared to previous cost of goods. Participation continues to expand rapidly, with over 1,600 screens already enrolled and purchasing through the Marketplace. ... janitorial supplies allows our members to reinvest in their guests, their employees, and their communities while ... “Independent cinemas are essential to the moviegoing experience, and we’re incredibly proud of the impact our ... Fandango. “Supporting the long-term strength and sustainability of independent cinemas remains a core priority for us, ... owners to reinvest where it matters most while creating exceptional experiences for their communities.” ... “Fandango ClubBuy’s expertise and scale give independent exhibitors access to savings and programs that would otherwise be out of reach. Their ability to leverage collective buying power across thousands of screens is truly remarkable, and the impact on our bottom line has been immediate and substantial. We couldn’t ask for a stronger partner in helping our members reduce costs and reinvest in the guest experience.” The ICA will continue to expand the Marketplace in 2026, growing the portfolio of programs and categories that help reduce operating expenses and strengthen margins for independent exhibitors of all sizes. As the Marketplace grows, purchasing volume, and the savings created by that volume, will continue to increase. The ICA’s partnership with ClubBuy is an important contributor to this success and will remain a key component of the Marketplace as more existing ICA members transition into the program and new independent exhibitors join the ICA.

headline: You Really Should Have Seen a Movie in a Private Cinema - Vogue
summary_text: Your All-Access Pass to Vogue DOWNLOAD NOW You Really Should Have Tried... # You Really Should Have Seen a Movie in a Private Cinema By Lisa Wong Macabasco Photo: Courtesy of Metro Private Cinema Are movies okay? It’s the question many have been asking this year—and the concern has only grown since the news broke earlier this month that Netflix, famously dismissive of the theatrical experience, would be acquiring the legendary Hollywood studio Warner Bros., which this year distributed a whopping eight films that topped the opening-weekend box office. By Grace Vance Published: Feb. 26, 2026 at 1:29 PM CST Email This Link Share on Facebook Share on X (formerly Twitter) ... CEDAR RAPIDS, Iowa (KCRG) - Iowa is launching a new film rebate program nearly two decades after its last incentive program ended in scandal. The two-year pilot program offers up to 30% back on qualifying expenses. To qualify, productions must come from Iowa studios, carry a budget of at least $1 million, film mostly in the state, and be made available to the public. The Iowa Economic Development Authority said the Renovo Media Group in Clear Lake, Iowa proposed the ... It is Iowa’s first major rebate program since the state abruptly ended its previous incentive program in 2009 after millions of dollars were misused. The state said the new program has more safeguards against fraud, including specifying allowable and non-allowable expenses. It also requires a production’s final budget be reviewed by a third-party before approval. Eric Dean Freese, director of the Cedar Rapids Independent Film Festival, said the program represents progress but leaves out Iowa’s independent filmmakers. “It’s baby steps,” Freese said. He said the new program is not competitive enough with other states. ... The new rebate program comes as the state recently put its Greenlight Grant for low-budget films on pause — ... The state said it will be evaluating the Greenlight Grant program “to ensure it has met the goals to advance Iowa’s ... Freese said the film represents Iowa’s growing industry. He said filmmakers just need more resources. ... The application window for the pilot rebate program is open through March 16, 2026. Funding runs through 2027. In a statement to TV9, the Iowa Economic Development Authority said it hopes the program will support large-scale ... So far, it says it’s received four applications for the rebate program. *Copyright 2026 KCRG. Klund said those projects ranged from feature films and documentaries to two dozen commercials shot across the state. She said the ease of filming keeps film crews coming back to the Show-Me State. “They’re able to hire the crews,” said Klund. “The support companies are here. They’re just enjoying ... Klund told Missourinet that Missouri seems to have become a hotspot for Christmas movies as well. “I would say that tax credit has something to do with it, but I also think that we’re in the Midwest, ... Klund said the tax credits showed a great return on investment for the state in 2025. “That’s 54 projects over the course of one year. So, we were very close to allocating the full cap. It’s 16 million that we have total. I do think in 2026, we will allocate all of our funding,” said Klund. In 2023, the Missouri Legislature passed and then-Gov. Mike Parson signed a bill into law to resurrect the offering of film tax credits. They are set to expire at the end of 2029, and Klund said she’s hopeful lawmakers will extend them. headline: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News
summary_text: Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections Reel Chicago News ****Menu Reel Chicago ****Menu Reel Chicago * Reel Indie # The Midwest Exchange joins Kinowerks in building creative connections * **By Reel Chicago * January 15, 2026 ** ** ** ** ### Share this: * Click to share on X (Opens in new window)X * Click to share on Facebook (Opens in new window)Facebook * Click to share on LinkedIn (Opens in new window)LinkedIn * Click to share on Mail (Opens in new window)Mail * Chicago’s creative community is about to gain a new kind of gathering space, one built around collaboration, sustainability, and shared stewardship.**The Midwest Exchange**, presented by**Kinowerks and Midwest Film Festival**, is launching as a values-driven initiative designed to keep professional creative resources in motion. The concept is simple but powerful: keeping equipment, materials, and knowledge from sitting idle or going to waste, and instead create a trusted environment where filmmakers and creative professionals can connect, exchange, and reinvest in one another. At its core, the Midwest Exchange emphasizes stewardship over ownership, sustainability in practice, professional care, and long-term investment in community. The goal is not just to host an event, but to strengthen the creative ecosystem itself. The partnership brings together two organizations with complementary strengths. Midwest Film Festival contributes more than two decades of nonprofit experience and credibility within the filmmaking community. Kinowerks provides purpose-built creative infrastructure, including screening rooms, studios, and thoughtfully designed event space tailored for production and collaboration. The Midwest Exchange is designed for working filmmakers, technicians, producers, directors, cinematographers, editors, post supervisors, theater and audio professionals, as well as emerging creatives committed to long-term careers. ... **The inaugural Midwest Exchange will take place February 21, 2026, from 12–4 p.m. at Kinowerks, 5645 North Ravenswood Avenue in Chicago.** Organizers estimate the initiative will reach a substantial regional audience, supported by a combined community that ... organizations’ programming. That built-in network gives the Exchange the potential to extend its impact well beyond a ... For more information or to get involved, contact**Grover Derupe atgrover.derupe@beesonventures.com**. **ALSO READ:** **31 Candles reaches theaters with Hannah Welever producing** 31 Candles ### Share this: * Click to share on X (Opens in new window)X ... * Click to share on Mail (Opens in new window)Mail * ### *Related* ... ... headline: Illinois strengthens film incentives as Gov. Pritzker signs SB 1911 Reel Chicago News
summary_text: Illinois strengthens film incentives as Gov. Pritzker signs SB 1911 Reel Chicago News ****Menu ****Menu * PRODUCTION CENTRAL # Illinois strengthens film incentives as Gov. Pritzker signs SB 1911 * **By Barbara Roche * December 12, 2025 ** ** ** ** ### Share this: * Twitter * Facebook * LinkedIn * Mail * Illinois filmmakers received a major boost today as Governor JB Pritzker signed SB 1911, a comprehensive update to the Illinois Film Production Services Tax Credit Act. The legislation, which passed during the General Assembly’s fall veto session, modernizes the state’s incentive structure, expands eligibility, and reinforces the industry’s long-term stability through 2039. The signing concludes a months-long push by industry advocates, including the Illinois Production Alliance (IPA), and solidifies Illinois’ ... Gov. Pritzker signed SB 1911 into law on December 12, 2025, enacting the most significant upgrade to the film tax credit in more than a decade. The strong vote margins reflect a bipartisan acknowledgement of the film industry’s statewide economic impact, from Chicago’s major studio infrastructure to the rapidly growing production activity in ... SB 1911 implements several changes designed to reduce production costs, strengthen local hiring, and give the Illinois ... The program’s extension through 2039 offers filmmakers, studios, and streaming partners the long-range certainty needed ... ### **Why This Matters for Illinois’ Production Ecosystem** Illinois has experienced significant production growth in recent years, generating more than $650 million in film and television spending in 2024 and tens of thousands of hires across crew, talent, and support sectors. The updated credit positions the state to retain and expand that momentum. For independent filmmakers, the bonus credits and broader expense categories make regional shoots and smaller-budget projects more attainable. For larger productions, including returning network series and streaming originals, the ... ### **Looking Ahead** With SB 1911 now law, Illinois enters 2026 with one of the strongest production incentive packages in the country. The enhancements reflect the industry’s evolving needs and demonstrate the state’s commitment to growing its film, television, and commercial sectors. **ALSO READ:** **Golden Globe taps Chicago productions with 4 nominations** SB 1911 ### Share this: * Twitter * Facebook * LinkedIn * Mail * ### *Related* ##### Share This: ** ** ** ** sign up * * * * * ... headline: Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms | Mint
summary_text: Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms | Mint View Market Dashboard News # Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms Dhirendra Kumar 3 min read2 Mar 2026, 05:31 AM IST The move marks a structural shift in India’s quality conformity assessment regime.(Mint) Summary Instead of relying heavily on prior on-site assessment, the revised framework places greater emphasis on documentation scrutiny, third-party laboratory testing and post-market surveillance, to support manufacturing expansion. New Delhi: In a regulatory overhaul aimed at easing compliance and supporting domestic manufacturing, the government has introduced a self-declaration route for select products under the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) framework, eliminating mandatory pre-licence factory inspections while tightening annual compliance requirements, two government officials aware of the matter said. summary_text: UFlex delivers resilient Q3; EBITDA jumps sequentially as macro tailwinds build ### UFlex delivers resilient Q3; EBITDA jumps sequentially as macro tailwinds build By: ICN Bureau Last updated : February 15, 2026 8:56 am The improvement came even as sales volumes declined 3.7% year-on-year to 151,245 MT Flexible packaging major UFlex has reported a resilient third quarter for FY26, posting strong sequential profitability gains despite pricing pressure, tariff uncertainty and GST-related disruption. Consolidated net revenue for Q3 FY26 stood at Rs. 3,632.9 crore, while normalized EBITDA rose to Rs. 4,39.5 crore, delivering a 12.1% margin — up 200 basis points quarter-on-quarter. Net profit came in at Rs. 48.6 crore. The improvement came even as sales volumes declined 3.7% year-on-year to 151,245 MT, reflecting softer realizations and ... 482,910 MT (+0.1% YoY). Net profit rebounded sharply to Rs. 121.1 crore, compared to a net loss of Rs. 26.2 crore in ... Commenting on the results, Ashok Chaturvedi, Chairman and Managing Director, UFlex Group, said: “The packaging industry remains on a strong growth path, supported by rising consumption, GST rationalisation, and an expanding organised retail sector, alongside a growing shift toward value-added and sustainable packaging solutions. Extended producer responsibility (EPR) regulations continue to play a key role in accelerating the industry’s transition toward sustainability across food, pharmaceuticals, FMCG, and consumer sectors." He added: "The recently presented Union Budget 2026–27, which focused on manufacturing, infrastructure, logistics, and exports, is expected to strengthen India’s packaging sector and will reinforce the country’s position as a global manufacturing hub. Additionally, India’s recent trade engagements with the EU and the US are expected to accelerate exports and create new opportunities for the domestic packaging industry by enabling faster technology adoption, ... "Our strategic expansion initiatives continue to progress well, with work on a greenfield aseptic packaging plant in ... Anantshree Chaturvedi, Vice Chairman & CEO, Flex Films International, added: “The outlook for the packaging industry remains strong. Recent trade frameworks are enhancing global supply chain efficiencies as tariff rationalization improves sourcing flexibility and competitiveness through 2026. "With operations spanning across India, Europe, Africa, the Middle East, and the Americas, UFlex continues to ensure ... With a diversified geographic mix — 56% international revenue and 44% domestic — UFlex is positioning itself to benefit ... Mexico. Despite global tariff volatility and pricing pressure, the company’s margin rebound and return to profitability over nine months signal strengthening fundamentals — and management is betting that macro tailwinds will accelerate growth in the quarters ahead. UFlex Launches Sustainable Water-Based Soft Touch Coating at PLASTINDIA 2026 X UFlex Limited, India’s largest multinational flexible packaging and solutions company, has launched its latest innovation from its chemical portfolio—FLEXCOAT Soft Touch Coating, an environmentally friendly, water-based soft-touch coating—at PLASTINDIA 2026. Developed to address the growing demand for premium, luxurious, and aesthetically appealing packaging, the coating is designed to enhance both tactile comfort and visual elegance across flexible packaging films as well as paper and paperboard substrates, making it a versatile solution for modern packaging applications. This product innovation strengthens their position as a low-risk supplier, supporting long-term procurement contracts with innovation incentives.

headline: TCPL Packaging Secures Rs 300 Crore Funding for Pan-India Box Manufacturing
summary_text: TCPL Packaging Ltd raises Rs 300 crore in venture debt to scale folding carton and rigid box manufacturing across 5 new plants in India. The funding bolsters financial stability, signaling low risk and capacity for volume commitments in supplier negotiations.

headline: Packport Packaging Risk Alert: Raw Material Shortage Hits Supplies
summary_text: Packport Packaging, supplier of custom corrugated boxes in Mumbai, warns of 20% production cuts due to kraft paper shortages from monsoon disruptions. Procurement teams should diversify sources and activate risk management clauses for alternative suppliers.

headline: Tilak Polypack Pvt. Ltd. - Redefining Packaging Solutions with Premium Films & Flexible Laminates
summary_text: Kalol, Gujarat, India, 2026-02-23 - /EPR Network/ - Tilak Polypack Pvt. Ltd., a leading name in the global packaging industry, proudly announces its continued commitment to delivering high-quality, innovative packaging solutions for a wide range of industrial and consumer applications. With a legacy spanning more than two decades, Tilak Polypack has established itself as a trusted manufacturer, supplier, and exporter of advanced flexible packaging products. Founded in 1995, Tilak Polypack has been at the forefront of the packaging materials sector, combining cutting-edge technology, premium food-grade raw materials, and stringent quality standards to serve clients across India and international markets. 🌟 Comprehensive Product Portfolio.

headline: Subam Papers Limited Schedules Board Meeting for February 25, 2026 to Review Q3FY26 Financial Results
summary_text: Subam Papers Limited has formally notified the BSE about an upcoming board meeting scheduled to review its quarterly financial performance. The Tamil Nadu - based packaging company will convene its board of directors on February 25, 2026, to deliberate on crucial financial matters for the third quarter of fiscal year 2026. Meeting Agenda and Financial Review. The board meeting has been specifically convened to consider and approve key financial documents for the quarter ended December 31, 2025. The primary agenda includes reviewing unaudited standalone and consolidated financial statements along with the accompanying Limited Review Report.

summary_text: Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms | Mint View Market Dashboard News # Govt amends BIS rules to quicken product certification, tighten compliance norms Dhirendra Kumar 3 min read2 Mar 2026, 05:31 AM IST The move marks a structural shift in India’s quality conformity assessment regime.(Mint) Summary Instead of relying heavily on prior on-site assessment, the revised framework places greater emphasis on documentation scrutiny, third-party laboratory testing and post-market surveillance, to support manufacturing expansion. New Delhi: In a regulatory overhaul aimed at easing compliance and supporting domestic manufacturing, the government has introduced a self-declaration route for select products under the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) framework, eliminating mandatory pre-licence factory inspections while tightening annual compliance requirements, two government officials aware of the matter said. headline: UFlex delivers resilient Q3; EBITDA jumps sequentially as macro tailwinds build
summary_text: UFlex delivers resilient Q3; EBITDA jumps sequentially as macro tailwinds build ### UFlex delivers resilient Q3; EBITDA jumps sequentially as macro tailwinds build By: ICN Bureau Last updated : February 15, 2026 8:56 am The improvement came even as sales volumes declined 3.7% year-on-year to 151,245 MT Flexible packaging major UFlex has reported a resilient third quarter for FY26, posting strong sequential profitability gains despite pricing pressure, tariff uncertainty and GST-related disruption. Consolidated net revenue for Q3 FY26 stood at Rs. 3,632.9 crore, while normalized EBITDA rose to Rs. 4,39.5 crore, delivering a 12.1% margin — up 200 basis points quarter-on-quarter. Net profit came in at Rs. 48.6 crore. The improvement came even as sales volumes declined 3.7% year-on-year to 151,245 MT, reflecting softer realizations and ... For the first nine months of FY26, revenue edged up 0.8% YoY to Rs. 11,415.7 crore, while volumes remained stable at 482,910 MT (+0.1% YoY). Net profit rebounded sharply to Rs. 121.1 crore, compared to a net loss of Rs. 26.2 crore in the same period last year — underscoring operational resilience amid macro headwinds and supply chain disruptions. Commenting on the results, Ashok Chaturvedi, Chairman and Managing Director, UFlex Group, said: “The packaging industry remains on a strong growth path, supported by rising consumption, GST rationalisation, and an expanding organised retail sector, alongside a growing shift toward value-added and sustainable packaging solutions. Extended producer responsibility (EPR) regulations continue to play a key role in accelerating the industry’s transition toward sustainability across food, pharmaceuticals, FMCG, and consumer sectors." He added: "The recently presented Union Budget 2026–27, which focused on manufacturing, infrastructure, logistics, and exports, is expected to strengthen India’s packaging sector and will reinforce the country’s position as a global manufacturing hub. Additionally, India’s recent trade engagements with the EU and the US are expected to accelerate exports and create new opportunities for the domestic packaging industry by enabling faster technology adoption, ... "Our strategic expansion initiatives continue to progress well, with work on a greenfield aseptic packaging plant in ... Anantshree Chaturvedi, Vice Chairman & CEO, Flex Films International, added: “The outlook for the packaging industry remains strong. Recent trade frameworks are enhancing global supply chain efficiencies as tariff rationalization ... "With operations spanning across India, Europe, Africa, the Middle East, and the Americas, UFlex continues to ensure ... With a diversified geographic mix — 56% international revenue and 44% domestic — UFlex is positioning itself to benefit ... Mexico. Despite global tariff volatility and pricing pressure, the company’s margin rebound and return to profitability over nine months signal strengthening fundamentals — and management is betting that macro tailwinds will accelerate growth in the quarters ahead. It aims to enable seamless, cross-border zero-emission trucking by building a reliable network of recharging/refuelling hubs suited for long-haul electric trucks, addressing one of the main barriers to widespread adoption of electric (and potentially hydrogen) trucks. The initiative targets the main heavy-freight corridors of the Trans-European Transport Network (TEN-T), starting with ... The initiative was formally launched in September 2025, when the signing of a Ministerial Declaration in Brussels ... draw up a detailed roadmap by March 2026, which will outline technical, regulatory, funding, and deployment measures. ... decarbonisation targets for transport. The first corridors are currently being piloted. ... needed. The infrastructure will consist of high-power recharging stations suitable for heavy-duty trucks —likely MCS (Megawatt Charging System) or similar, per technical ambition —and supporting grid enhancements, faster permitting and ... **What will this mean for the European CV sector?** For the European CV sector, the Clean Transport Corridor Initiative is a potentially transformative development, offering a credible foundation for long-haul zero-emission trucking. By enabling cross-border charging/refuelling, it removes one of the major obstacles for fleet operators considering a transition to electric or hydrogen trucks: ... invest in zero-emission trucks, knowing that recharging/refuelling access will exist throughout their routes. This will ... On a strategic level, CTCI could reshape how logistics is done across Europe, enabling more sustainable, cleaner ... test. summary_text: Coopesa Clears Hurdles For Facility Move | Aviation Week Network ## Share # Coopesa Clears Hurdles For Facility Move ## Share Lee Ann Shay January 29, 2026 Coopesa’s existing hangars in Costa Rica. Credit: Lee Ann Shay/Aviation Week Network SANTIAGO, Chile—The Costa Rican MRO Coopesa received long-awaited approval to move its facility at the Juan Santamaria (San Jose) International Airport, with groundbreaking scheduled for April, according to its CEO. The new facility will double capacity and provide new-build hangars to replace its early 1960s ones. The new facility with two hangars will feature 16 bays—up from its current eight. One hangar will accommodate widebody aircraft, which will expand Coopesa’s service portfolio at a time when global widebody maintenance demand exceeds ... Coopesa CEO Rimsky Buitrago tells Aviation Week he expects the facility to open in early 2029. ... relocating from its existing facilities to the new ones. During the switch, “we will work in parallel at the old ... widebody capabilities and a much more state-of-the-art base.” Buitrago has been driving a parallel strategy of building new facilities, new information technology systems and operational efficiencies since returning as Coopesa CEO in 2023. The MRO uses EmpowerMX’s FleetCycle for maintenance control, as well as internally developed systems, and has been integrating them as part of is paperless MRO initiative. When asked about the investment total, Buitrago said he couldn’t disclose that, “but you can imagine it’s a lot of money.” He says, “we have the cash flow [from] several financial partners.” To accommodate the expansion, Coopesa will need to add 400 mechanics to its existing 600, says the executive. The independent MRO planned to start constructing the new hangars in 2012 but was delayed first due to government decisions, then because of the COVID-19 pandemic. The push post-pandemic became more urgent because the Juan Santamaria International Airport wants to expand the passenger terminal to where Coopesa is currently ... “This is a national goal, and I think we have all the stars aligned right now to do the expansion of the airport, relocating Coopesa and doubling the MRO capacity to take advantage of the excess demand of MRO services,” Buitrago ... headline: Why Brazil's Cargo-First eVTOL Strategy Makes Sense (And Where It Doesn't)
summary_text: Why Brazil's Cargo-First eVTOL Strategy Makes Sense (And Where It Doesn't) Eve Air Mobility is chasing 2,900 passenger eVTOL orders worth $14.5 billion and finally got its prototype airborne in December 2025, but two smaller Brazilian companies made different bets years ago. Moya Aero and Speedbird Aero figured out that hauling boxes beats hauling people when you need to prove autonomous electric aircraft won’t fall out of the ... February 2024, Speedbird earned exemption from ANAC’s Article E94.103(a), making it the first Latin American operator ... Speedbird operates routes for Grupo Fleury and Grupo Hermes Pardini, moving medical supplies across El Salvador through ... blood samples, vaccines, and organs for Grupo Fleury and Grupo Hermes Pardini. These companies prove something important: cargo operations skip the public skepticism problem. A drone crash without a ... ### São José dos Campos Advantage Brazil’s aerospace cluster matters here. São José dos Campos, 90 kilometers northeast of São Paulo, hosts Embraer, dozens of suppliers, and a concentration of aeronautical engineering talent. The ecosystem knows how to certify aircraft. ANAC maintains bilateral agreements with the FAA and EASA. What Brazil certifies carries weight ... Speedbird’s DLV-1 certification came through ANAC’s experimental aircraft pathway, but regulators still required over a ... lifted off on December 19, 2025, at Embraer’s Gavião Peixoto facility, it validated eight fixed-pitch lifter rotors and ... Speedbird’s drones can carry up to 8 kg on the DLV-2. Moya’s aircraft handles 200 kilograms. These payload capacities ... Geography creates another gap. Brazil’s Amazon region, where Speedbird operates some routes, presents natural use cases ... hasn’t changed meaningfully since 2020. Solid-state batteries promise improvements but remain perpetually five years away. Joby Aviation’s hydrogen-electric demonstrator flew 561 miles in September 2024, but that hybrid approach adds ... ### What Brazil Actually Proves The cargo-first strategy demonstrates regulatory pathway viability, not market viability. Speedbird proved that ANAC will certify autonomous aircraft for commercial operations. Moya proved operators can attract customer commitments (100+ letters of intent) for cargo eVTOLs before certification is complete. This matters for other companies navigating certification in different jurisdictions. Brazil also proves that geographic and economic conditions create natural markets for autonomous cargo aircraft independent of passenger operations. Medical logistics in cities like Salvador, where traffic congestion costs hours and lives, justify the economics of drone delivery. Agricultural operations in Mato Grosso, where Moya has five-unit ... But these use cases don’t directly translate to the urban air mobility envisioned by Eve, Joby, Archer, or Volocopter. Cargo operations at 120 meters altitude with eight-kilogram payloads operate in different airspace, different noise profiles, and different cost structures than passenger eVTOLs carrying 400-kilogram payloads at 450-1,200 meters. The Brazilian approach builds confidence in autonomous systems. It accumulates operational data. It validates some regulatory approaches. It doesn’t prove passenger UAM will work economically or that the public will accept it at scale. Eve’s 2,900 orders represent customer interest, not committed revenue. Lilium’s November 2024 insolvency, after burning through substantial capital, proves that impressive order books don’t guarantee survival. The cargo-first companies in Brazil stay smaller, leaner, focused on near-term revenue. They’ll reach profitability faster than any passenger eVTOL operator. Whether that makes them a model for the broader LAE industry depends on the specific challenge companies face. For proving autonomous electric aircraft work safely and economically, cargo-first wins. headline: LATAM Airlines Launches MRO Training Partnership with INACAP in Chile
summary_text: LATAM Airlines has partnered with INACAP Technological University in Chile for a six-month specialized aviation maintenance technician training program. Starting in March, graduates from industrial maintenance and mechatronics programs will receive practical training at LATAM’s MRO workshops in Pudahuel, Chile, supporting supplier development, skill risk management, and long-term procurement strategies in South America.

summary_text: Market Sector + Aerospace + Air Transport + MRO + Defense + Space + Business Aviation Markets [...] “The facility we have built is state-of-the-art, and there’s nothing like it in Latin America. We are way ahead of where we envisioned we would be at this time,” Asenscio says. “Our goal is to be the biggest and most efficient MRO facility in Latin America. Our growth in three years has been more than what anyone expected.” APAS Chile currently holds maintenance approvals from regulators in Chile, Argentina, Colombia, the Dominican Republic, Mexico and the U.S. It is also working to obtain European Union Aviation Safety Agency approval due to interest from Turkish Airlines. “We’re young, we’re the baby in the industry, but people are hearing about us, and Turkish [Airlines] is interested in talking to us, so there’s an international demand that’s beginning to come to us.” [...] Lindsay Bjerregaard Lindsay Bjerregaard is managing editor for Aviation Week’s MRO portfoli

headline: Flexible packaging material markets see price volatility continuing into Q1 2025 - Flexible Packaging Europe
summary_text: Flexible packaging material markets see price volatility continuing into Q1 2025 - Flexible Packaging Europe # Flexible packaging material markets see price volatility continuing into Q1 2025 The mixed bag of price fluctuations seen at the end of 2024 continued into the first three months of 2025. Polyethylene led the way in price increases as both HDPE and LDPE price increased by 3% and 4% respectively vs. Q4 2024, taking them to levels not seen since early 2023. Conversely 60 gsm on-side coated paper and 12-micron PET saw declines of -3% and -2% respectively vs. Q4 2024. Alufoil (7 micron) continued its upward trend, with a 3% increase, while BOPA film (15-micron) gained a modest 2% and BOPP (20-micron) was stable, according to figures from Flexible Packaging Europe (FPE). Senior Research Analyst Santiago Castro of Wood Mackenzie added some context to these figures, “The prices paid in Europe for flexible packaging materials varied in Q1 2025 were driven by price fluctuations in raw materials, logistics issues and factors in overseas markets. The BOPET price reductions were largely due to declines in raw materials. While the reduction in paper prices is accounted for by under-utilised mill capacity and lower-than-expected energy prices. Higher raw material prices account for Alufoil and BOPA increases. BOPP prices remained relatively unchanged because of continued weak demand and over-supply. “Overall demand for flexible packaging remained weak in Q1, driven by reduced consumer spending and a shift towards leaner inventory management. Prices are expected to increase for all substrates, largely due to increased raw materials ... Kaushik Mitra, Chemical Market Analyst on LDPE and HDPE continued, “The first quarter sharp increase in PE prices is ... affected by low order booking and supply chain bottlenecks. PE demand is still lacklustre due to macroeconomic factors ... signs of stability. “Potential EU tariffs on PE from 12th April caused market volatility and nervousness as, even though there is huge ... normalising later in the year.” Adding his perspective on the figures Guido Aufdenkamp of FPE said, “While raw material supply and logistics issues continue to weigh on flexible packaging markets, there are signs that after the weak 2023 and recovery in 2024 the overall demand continues to increase slightly.. headline: Prices of flexible packaging materials in Europe steady on high levels in Q4 2023 - Flexible Packaging Europe
summary_text: Prices of flexible packaging materials in Europe steady on high levels in Q4 2023 - Flexible Packaging Europe # Prices of flexible packaging materials in Europe steady on high levels in Q4 2023 Although destocking ends in Q4 2023 demand is stabilising in 2024. The final three months of 2023 saw a mixed set of results in prices for the basket of flexible packaging materials used to assess markets across Europe. While there were gains in the price of HDPE, up 7% on the previous quarter and LDPE, up 8% in the same period, other results were patchier. The continuing weakness in demand and now the volatile situation in the Middle East, adding to the Ukraine war uncertainties, have had a negative impact on the outlook for 2024. There was a modest decline in the price of 7-micron aluminium foil (4%). Elsewhere 60gsm one-sided coated paper fell 7% against the Q3 figures while both BOPP 20-micron film and12-micron PET film remained unchanged. A modest rise of 1% for 15-micron BOPA film completes the end of year figures. While prices continue to drop or stabilize most are still well above the benchmark price from Q4 2020. 60gsm one sided coated paper, 15-mircon BOPA film, 12-micron PET film, HDPE and LDPE generally remain between 30-40% above that benchmark, and aluminium foil remains 60% higher. Only 20-micron BOPP film is returning towards the 2020 measure, at 13% above that price. However, all these prices are well off the peaks seen in mid-2022. “Prices paid in Europe for flexible packaging materials were uneven in Q4. They decreased for both alufoil and one-side coated paper. Alufoil price declines were mainly due to conversion cost reductions. Paper pricing tended to be stable for small and medium-size customers, while some large buyers agreed reductions,” according to Santiago Castro,Senior Research Analyst, Films and Flexible Packaging at Wood Mackenzie. “For BOPET and BOPP, prices remained stable on average. With demand flat, some attempts by suppliers to increase prices were rebuffed. BOPA prices rose marginally in Q4, driven mainly by increases in the price of PA6 resin,” he continued. “As for Q1 2024, consumer demand is expected to remain subdued. Inventory rebalancing has mostly ended. The situation in the Red Sea is putting pressure on imported raw materials, which could well drive prices up.” Adding his views about the latest figures Guido Aufdenkamp, Executive Director of FPE commented, “The combination of very high stocks along the supply chain and pressure on end-consumer demand due to high inflation was not favourable for flexible packaging manufacturers and caused a decline of deliveries in 2023. There is continuous uncertainty due to the various conflicts in and around Europe and throughout the world bring, but recovery of the European economy, ... The industry is cautious optimistic for 2024 when comparing the demand of flexible packaging with last year.” ## Downloads - European-Price-Index_Q4-2023.jpg (807.8 KiB) - Italian Version (344.2 KiB) - French Version (299.0 KiB) - German Version (260.8 KiB) - English Version (262.1 KiB) - [

summary_text: OKTO PAYMENTS strengthens expansion with appointment of new GM for Northern Latin America - G3 Newswire Skip to Content Monday 2nd March 2026 Other languages Supplier News # OKTO PAYMENTS strengthens expansion with appointment of new GM for Northern Latin America By Phil Martin - 2 March 2026 9:56 AM - Last updated on 2 March 2026 9:56 AM OKTO's César Rodríguez Otaola OKTO's César Rodríguez Otaola ## OKTO appoints in Northern Lat Am in response to the rapidly evolving digital payments landscape OKTO PAYMENTS is strengthening its growth strategy across Latin America. The company has announced the appointment of César Rodríguez Otaola as General Manager for Northern Latin America, where he will lead operations in Peru and Mexico, with future expansion plans across Colombia, Ecuador, Venezuela, Central America, and the Caribbean. Rodríguez brings over 15 years of experience in the payments and financial services industry, with a strong track record in business strategy, market expansion, and regional banking partnerships. Throughout his career, he has held senior leadership roles at PayRetailers, Paysafe, SafetyPay, and American Express, actively contributing to the ... “Latin America is a strategic growth region for the company, and strengthening our leadership presence locally is a key ... Latin America’s digital payments ecosystem is expanding rapidly, driven by accelerated mobile adoption, real-time payment infrastructure, and increased demand from digital commerce and entertainment platforms. Legacy film and TV producers like Paramount and Warner Bros. have launched their own streaming businesses, but lack the ... Paramount’s offer includes Warner Bros. cable-TV networks like CNN and TNT. The company kicked off the bidding with a private offer in September. That was just one month after Ellison closed on the merger of his Skydance Media with Paramount, giving him control of the Paramount film studio, streaming service and TV networks like CBS and MTV. Warner Bros. began soliciting offers for the business in October before finalizing a deal with Netflix in December. summary_text: DCDC Acquires Full Ownership of CineCert - Celluloid Junkie ## DCDC Acquires Full Ownership of CineCert Los Angeles ( February 16, 2026 ) - Digital Cinema Distribution Coalition (DCDC) today announced that it has acquired the remaining ownership interest in CineCert, taking full control of the company following its strategic investment last year. The transaction further strengthens DCDC’s commitment to supporting essential digital cinema technologies that underpin secure and reliable content distribution. CineCert, along with its Clipster mastering solution, has long been recognized as the gold standard for KDM management and digital cinema mastering, serving leading studios and post-production facilities across the globe. Since DCDC’s initial investment in 2025, the companies have worked closely to ensure continued innovation, operational stability, ... By bringing CineCert fully into the DCDC organization, the coalition reinforces its mission as an industry-owned utility dedicated to the health, security, and advancement of the digital cinema ecosystem. The acquisition ensures that CineCert and Clipster will continue to evolve in step with the needs of studios, distributors, and exhibitors. ... John Hurst, CTO of CineCert, added, “The support of DCDC will help ensure our engineering team continues to develop a strong long- term roadmap, security enhancements, and next-generation mastering capabilities. Together, we can move ... CineCert will continue to operate with its existing team and product focus, while benefiting from DCDC’s industry stewardship and long-term strategic support. About DCDC Digital Cinema Distribution Coalition (DCDC) is a leading provider of digital content distribution solutions for the cinema industry with a footprint of over 3,200 sites and more than 33,000 screens across the U.S. With an extensive network of connected cinemas, DCDC ensures efficient, secure, and reliable delivery of feature films, trailers, and advertisements. For further information, please contact dcdcnetwork@dcdcnetwork.com About CineCert Since 2005, CineCert has provided software solutions and consulting services to post-production companies, motion picture studios, cinema distribution networks and other organizations that depend on the precise implementation of digital cinema and IMF technology. CineCert’s products are well known for their reliability and adaptability to both automated workflows and cloud-friendly applications. CineCert is based in Burbank, California.

headline: Mining House, Denison Mines, B2Gold Stockhouse Trends - Farmonaut
summary_text: ✔ Rising Demand: Gold, uranium, lithium, rare earths, and copper top the list of coveted minerals for energy transition and digital infrastructure worldwide. ✔ Regulation & ESG: Regulatory oversight continues to tighten as shareholder activism around environmental, social, and governance issues accelerates. 📊 Data Insight: Platforms like Denison Mines Stockhouse and B2Gold Stockhouse transform access to operational, financial, and news data for a broader investor audience. ⚠ Price Volatility: Gold and uranium experience heightened price swings due to macroeconomic uncertainty, energy policy, and strategic stockpiling. ✔ Sustainability Imperative: Success in mining now hinges as much on environmental stewardship as on production efficiency. #### Key Global Mining Drivers in 2025 [...] As digitalization continues to transform industry engagement, stockhouse platforms empower a new generation of investors—emphasizing not only profitability but responsibility and growth potential. ##
